title: Muddy Vietnamese Quinoa Canh Chua
description: A funky, shaggy one-pot Vietnamese quinoa canh chua featuring mixed grains, baked tofu, and a tangy tamarind broth, served on a hot-stone plate.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup quinoa, 1 cup brown rice, and 1/2 cup millet under cold water until clear.
In a large saucepan, heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil over medium heat and sauté the grain blend for 2 minutes.
Add 400 ml coconut milk, 1 tbsp tamarind paste, 2 tbsp lime juice, and 1 tsp sugar to the saucepan.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the grains cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 30 minutes, add 200 g cubed tofu and 50 g sliced tempeh, and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste, ensuring the flavors balance.
Remove from heat and transfer the hot stew into a preheated hot-stone plate to keep it warm during serving.
Garnish with chopped scallions before serving.
Serve the Muddy Vietnamese Quinoa Canh Chua hot, letting diners enjoy the funky, shaggy texture.
